description: Pre-selected quality compounders for bear market deployment
deployment_philosophy: Select during bull markets, buy during bear markets when 8+ indicators trigger. Heavy deployment, not DCA.

categories.name: Vertical Market Software
categories.rationale: High switching costs, recurring revenue, capital-light compounding. Fisher quality + Lindy durability.

positions.name: Constellation Software
positions.thesis: VMS roll-up pioneer. 30-year track record of disciplined M&A at 25%+ ROIC. Burgiss, Lerro leadership. Decentralized operating model. Quality compounds forever.
positions.entry_trigger: 8+ indicators or CSU down >25% from peak

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects CSU to grow FCF at 12-14% annually for 10 years, then 3% perpetually.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If multiple compresses from 35x to 25x over 10 years, need 16-18% FCF growth just to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. VMS TAM expanding (cloud migration, vertical deepening). $50B+ addressable.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Historical 20%+ FCF growth (2010-2020). M&A pipeline remains robust. Pricing power intact.
three_ps.probable: 15-18% FCF CAGR. Base case: 15%. Bull case: 18%. Track record supports this.
three_ps.assessment: Probable (15-18%) > Implied (12-14%). Stock is reasonably priced, not expensive.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Price < CAD 2,200 (Probable growth 20%+ above Implied) OR 8+ bear signals
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > CAD 3,500 (Implied > 20%, multiple >40x) AND Probable cannot support it
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: HOLD (slightly undervalued, wait for 8+ bear signals or deeper dip)

positions.name: Topicus.com
positions.thesis: CSU spinoff focused on European VMS. Robin van Poelje leadership. Same playbook, earlier stage, smaller scale. Geographic diversification from CSU.

positions.name: Lumine Group
positions.thesis: CSU spinoff focused on communication/media VMS. Smaller than TOI, higher risk. Portfolio diversification play.
positions.notes: Already at target weight. Monitor only.

categories.name: Enduring Luxury
categories.rationale: Lindy effect: survived centuries. Pricing power, brand moats, generational wealth preservation. Anti-fragile to inflation.

positions.name: Hermès International
positions.thesis: 300+ year family business. Birkin bags, silk scarves. Supply-constrained luxury (artisan bottleneck = pricing power). 30%+ ROIC, no debt. Lindy king.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 8-10% EPS growth for 10 years at premium multiple (luxury scarcity premium).

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If multiple compresses from 55x to 40x, need 12-14% EPS growth to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. Ultra-high-net-worth (UHNW) wealth growing 6-8% annually. China/Asia demand underpenetrated.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Historical 10-12% revenue growth, margin expansion from pricing power. Artisan constraint limits volume.
three_ps.probable: 10-12% EPS CAGR. Pricing power + slow volume expansion. Track record: 11% revenue CAGR (2010-2020).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (10-12%) ≈ Implied (8-10%). Fairly valued at current multiple.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Price < EUR 2,000 (Probable > Implied by 20%+) OR 8+ bear signals + price < EUR 2,200
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > EUR 3,000 (P/E >65x, Implied >12%, unsustainable for slow-growth luxury)
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: HOLD (fairly valued, wait for 8+ bear signals for entry)

positions.name: Ferrari
positions.thesis: Luxury automaker (not car company). Artificial scarcity, 2-year waitlists. 35%+ EBIT margins. Pricing power > volume growth. Survived 75+ years.

positions.name: Exor N.V.
positions.thesis: Holding company: Ferrari, Stellantis, Iveco, CNH, Juventus, The Economist, PartnerRe. Discount to NAV (~20-30%). Agnelli family skin in game.
positions.entry_trigger: 9+ indicators (holding company discount needs stronger signal)
positions.notes: Already above target. Monitor discount to NAV.

categories.name: Technology Compounders
categories.rationale: Wide moats, network effects, high ROIC. Dominant platforms with pricing power.

positions.name: Alphabet Inc.
positions.thesis: Search monopoly (90% market share). YouTube moat. Cloud #3. AI positioning. 30%+ ROIC, fortress balance sheet. Survived 25+ years.

positions.name: Adobe Inc.
positions.thesis: Creative software monopoly. Subscription model lock-in. 35%+ ROIC. Photoshop/Illustrator = industry standard for 30+ years.

positions.name: Microsoft
positions.thesis: Enterprise moat (Office, Azure, Windows). 40%+ ROIC. OpenAI partnership. Survived 50+ years, still compounding.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 12-14% EPS growth for 10 years, driven by Azure + AI monetization.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If multiple compresses from 38x to 28x (mature tech), need 16-18% EPS growth to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. Cloud TAM $1T+. Enterprise software moat defensible. AI tailwinds real.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Azure growing 25-30%. Office/Windows stable cash cows. AI integration across product suite.
three_ps.probable: 14-16% EPS CAGR. Base case: 14% (Azure moderates, margin expansion). Bull case: 16% (AI accelerates).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (14-16%) ≈ Implied (12-14%). Fairly to slightly overvalued at current multiple.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Price < USD 380 (Probable >20% above Implied) OR 8+ bear signals + price < USD 420
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > USD 550 (P/E >45x, Implied >18%, difficult to sustain for mega-cap)
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: WAIT (fairly valued, not a buy unless 8+ bear signals trigger)

categories.name: Broad Market Exposure
categories.rationale: Core recovery plays. Get the beta, reduce single-stock risk. Safe deployment when signals fire.

positions.name: Vanguard FTSE All-World ETF
positions.thesis: Global market cap weighted. Lowest cost, broadest diversification. US + ex-US exposure. Mean reversion to long-term growth.

positions.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF
positions.thesis: US large-cap exposure. Complement to VWRA. Mean reversion play when market oversold.

positions.name: Invesco QQQ Trust
positions.thesis: Tech-heavy Nasdaq 100. Higher beta, bigger recoveries. Buy during panic for outsized gains.

categories.name: Other Compounders
categories.rationale: Opportunistic high-quality names. Add when thesis + signals align.

positions.name: Berkshire Hathaway
positions.thesis: Buffett fortress. Insurance float, diversified holdings. Survives everything. Buy when trading at discount to intrinsic value.

positions.name: Costco Wholesale
positions.thesis: Membership moat. 90%+ renewal rates. Low-margin, high-volume model = pricing power during inflation. 40-year track record.

deployment_notes: Total target allocation: 100% when fully deployed
Dry powder target: Deploy when 8+ bear market indicators trigger
Deployment style: Heavy, concentrated buys. Not DCA.
Rebalancing: No. This is additive deployment of new cash only.
Exit strategy: Hold forever (or until thesis breaks). Not selling during bear markets.
